title: Köpenick: Quick Summary
items: **Köpenick is older than Berlin itself**: first recorded in 1209 as Copnic, almost 30 years before Berlin and Cölln appear in any document. Its old town sits on an island where the Dahme meets the Spree, in the far southeast of the city.

**Getting there is easy: take the S3 to Köpenick**, about 20 minutes from Alexanderplatz, then bus 164 for about nine minutes to Schloßplatz, or walk Bahnhofstraße in about 20 minutes. One normal AB ticket covers the S-Bahn, the bus, the trams and even the little F12 ferry.

**Schloss Köpenick costs 8 euros (reduced 4)** and is closed Monday and Tuesday. Summer hours are Wed-Fri 11:00-17:00 and Sat-Sun 11:00-18:00. The palace island and its garden are free to walk any day.

**The town hall is the site of Germany’s most famous con**: in October 1906 an unemployed shoemaker in a second-hand captain’s uniform occupied Rathaus Köpenick with real soldiers and walked out with the town cash box. His bronze statue still waits by the entrance.

**The Müggelsee, Berlin’s largest lake, is a short S-Bahn ride away**: continue east to Friedrichshagen and walk Bölschestraße down to the water. The Müggelturm tower nearby costs 6 euros for 126 steps of lake views, but its opening hours are short, so check ahead.
